en: How do you go to the block-house?

ab: wa’engtingi kake na sangepae mwa ta’oanane.
en: I drive to the block-house.
How does he go to the hospital?
He walks to the hospital.
How do you go to Penghu?
I go to Penghu by plane.
How do you go to Penghu?
I go to Penghu by boat.
How do you go home?
I go home by MRT.
How does ’Igeane go to school?
’Igeane goes to school by train.

ab: Tangooco ka mani ’inonini no mwani kiwkay?
en: How does Tangooco go to church?
Tangooco goes to church by bicycle.
Do you go to school by MRT?
No, I go to school by bus.
Do you go to school by bicycle?
Yes, I go to school by bicycle.
Do you go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail?

ab: esi, ’iyanono kake na kaw-tyi mwa Takao.
en: Yes, I go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail.

ch: A:你去台東搭火車還是搭飛機?
en: Are you going to Taitung by train or by plane?

ab: ’angohikoki kake, ’angohikokimade ka maidai kimade na kisya.
ch: B:我搭飛機，搭飛機比搭火車快。
en: I will take the plane because it is faster than the train.

ab: mwaso kiwkay ka ’angobase nyaso? wa’engetingi nyaso na sangepae?
ch: A:你去教會搭公車還是開車?
en: Are you going to drive to the church or take the bus?

ab: wa’engtingi kake na sangepae, ’engtingimade na sangepae ka maidai kimade na base.
ch: B:我開車去教會，開車比搭公車方便。
en: I will drive to the church because it is more convenient than the bus.
Is the market close to your house?

ch: B:不近，我都騎摩托車去超市。
en: No, it is not, so I always ride the motorcycle to the market.
Is the post office close to the school?
Yes, it is within walking distance.

ab: takanakanane na do’o ka wade’ethe nyani tisyaba?
en: Is the restaurant close to the station?

ab: mobalatimade tisyaba ka wadavadavace kimade ’aima na penkang lakela.
ch: B:出車站再走五分鐘就到了。
en: It only takes five minutes after you walk out of the station.
